commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh And at Taberah, and at Massah, and at Kibrothhattaavah, you provoked the LORD to wrath. Burning The places called in Hebrew, "Tab era, Masa, and Ki broth Hatta Avah. "(Haydock) At the first, the murmurers were burnt; (Numbers xi. 1) at the second, or at Raphidim, (Calmet) the people demanded water, and were supplied from Horeb; (Exodus xvii. ; Menochius) though some confound this with the former place. It seems rather to refer to the temptation, or murmur of the people, on account of quails, Numbers xi. , and Psalm lxxvii.
